title: HTTP Semantics, Roles, Representations, and Layered Evidence · Automation and Data Client S… · Language Clinic: Stateless Application State
summary: A civic-data importer calls several public catalogs, but one catch-all exception erases DNS, TLS, status, decode, and data-quality evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http py stateless application state increment in a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers protocol evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain protocol statelessness while locating cookies, tokens, caches, and workflow state outside core request semantics. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Trace request construction, routing, transport, intermediary handling, response interpretation, and cleanup as distinct stages. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")
